system: You are proposing baseline QUALITY GATES for a software system, given a list of its observable signals. A gate is a metric over one or more signals that is worth monitoring. Do NOT set any threshold/target number (a human will). For each gate give a compute type from [rate, ratio, p95, psi, count, mean], a direction ('min' = higher-is-better, 'max' = lower-is-better), and 'inputs' = the list of signal keys it is computed from. Return JSON: {"gates":[{"key","description","direction","compute","inputs","segment_dimension","rationale"}]}.
raw_response: {"gates":[{"key":"availability.healthcheck_success_rate","description":"Proportion of health check requests that return 200 OK, indicating basic service availability.","direction":"min","compute":"rate","inputs":["http.health.200"],"segment_dimension":"environment","rationale":"Health endpoint is the simplest liveness signal; tracking its success rate catches outages, routing issues, and dependency failures early."},{"key":"reliability.global_500_rate","description":"Rate of unhandled 500 Internal Server Error responses across all requests.","direction":"max","compute":"rate","inputs":["http.global_error.500"],"segment_dimension":"environment","rationale":"Unhandled 500s represent unexpected failures; monitoring their rate is a core reliability gate and a leading indicator of regressions."},{"key":"todos.read_list_success_rate","description":"Rate of successful list-todos responses (200 OK).","direction":"min","compute":"rate","inputs":["http.todos.list.200"],"segment_dimension":"environment","rationale":"List is a primary read path; its success rate reflects API availability and data-access health for common usage."},{"key":"todos.read_get_success_to_notfound_ratio","description":"Ratio of successful get-todo responses (200) to not-found responses (404).","direction":"min","compute":"ratio","inputs":["http.todos.get.200","http.todos.get.404"],"segment_dimension":"environment","rationale":"A shifting balance between found vs not-found can indicate client misuse, ID generation issues, data loss, or routing problems; ratio is more informative than either count alone."},{"key":"todos.create_success_rate","description":"Rate of successful create-todo responses (201 Created).","direction":"min","compute":"rate","inputs":["http.todos.create.201"],"segment_dimension":"environment","rationale":"Create is a core write path; success rate captures end-to-end correctness of validation, persistence, and response formatting."},{"key":"todos.create_validation_error_ratio_missing_title","description":"Ratio of create validation failures due to missing/blank title (400) relative to successful creates (201).","direction":"max","compute":"ratio","inputs":["http.todos.create.400.missing_title","http.todos.create.201"],"segment_dimension":"environment","rationale":"Tracks client-side input quality and potential UX/API contract issues; spikes can indicate breaking changes or frontend regressions."},{"key":"todos.update_success_rate","description":"Rate of successful update-todo responses (200 OK).","direction":"min","compute":"rate","inputs":["http.todos.update.200"],"segment_dimension":"environment","rationale":"Update is a core mutation path; monitoring success rate helps detect persistence failures and contract mismatches."},{"key":"todos.update_no_fields_error_ratio","description":"Ratio of update requests rejected for having no updatable fields (400) relative to successful updates (200).","direction":"max","compute":"ratio","inputs":["http.todos.update.400.no_fields","http.todos.update.200"],"segment_dimension":"environment","rationale":"High rates suggest client bugs or schema drift (e.g., sending empty bodies); ratio normalizes by traffic volume."},{"key":"todos.update_notfound_ratio","description":"Ratio of update not-found responses (404) relative to successful updates (200).","direction":"max","compute":"ratio","inputs":["http.todos.update.404","http.todos.update.200"],"segment_dimension":"environment","rationale":"Elevated not-found on update can indicate stale client state, ID issues, or data consistency problems; ratio highlights changes over time."},{"key":"todos.delete_success_rate","description":"Rate of successful delete-todo responses (204 No Content).","direction":"min","compute":"rate","inputs":["http.todos.delete.204"],"segment_dimension":"environment","rationale":"Delete is a core lifecycle operation; success rate detects failures in persistence and authorization/routing (if added later)."},{"key":"todos.delete_notfound_ratio","description":"Ratio of delete not-found responses (404) relative to successful deletes (204).","direction":"max","compute":"ratio","inputs":["http.todos.delete.404","http.todos.delete.204"],"segment_dimension":"environment","rationale":"Helps detect client attempts to delete non-existent resources, stale UI state, or data integrity issues; ratio is stable across traffic changes."},{"key":"startup.port_logged_count","description":"Count of backend startups that log the port they are running on.","direction":"min","compute":"count","inputs":["log.backend_startup.port"],"segment_dimension":"environment","rationale":"Ensures startup observability is present; missing logs can indicate startup failures, logging misconfiguration, or deployment issues."},{"key":"startup.port_discovery_file_write_count","description":"Count of startups where the backend writes the port discovery file for frontend integration.","direction":"min","compute":"count","inputs":["file.backend_port_discovery"],"segment_dimension":"environment","rationale":"Validates the integration contract for frontend discovery; drops can indicate filesystem permission/path issues or changes that break local/dev orchestration."}]}
